name: Natco
description_short: Natco Credit Union is a state-chartered Indiana credit union established in 1945, serving 17,200+ members with $151.8M in assets across 1 branch and 3 ATMs.
description_long:
Natco Credit Union was established in 1945 and is headquartered in Richmond, Indiana, operating under a state charter. The institution has grown to serve over 17,200 members and stewards more than $151.8 million in assets, supported by approximately 72 dedicated employees as of 2026. As a traditional credit union, Natco maintains a physical footprint with 1 main branch and 3 ATMs in the Richmond area, including a location in Connersville based on member reviews.

Natco offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, business loans, mortgages, and IRA/KEOGH accounts. The credit union provides both traditional banking services and modern digital capabilities, including online banking, mobile app access for iOS and Android, mobile payments, bill pay, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. Members can access financial counseling, financial education workshops, tax preparation assistance, and financial calculators to support their financial planning.

Natco distinguishes itself as a member-focused credit union with deep local roots in Indiana and a commitment to personalized service. The institution offers membership eligibility under its state charter and provides mortgage options including fixed-rate mortgages (15 years or less, and over 15 years) and adjustable-rate mortgages. The credit union maintains an active community Q&A forum and publishes financial wellness tips to educate members.

However, Natco has limited geographic reach with only 1 branch and 3 ATMs, which may constrain accessibility for members outside the Richmond area. Member reviews are mixed, with one reviewer praising the helpful staff at both Connersville and Richmond locations, while another reported serious issues with money handling and rude staff. The small branch network and mixed reviews suggest potential service consistency issues that prospective members should investigate before joining. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mortgages (fixed-rate 15 years or less, fixed-rate over 15 years, adjustable-rate)
Online banking with bill pay and e-statements
Mobile payments and remote deposit capture
Financial counseling and financial education workshops
pros: Established institution with 79+ years of operating history (founded 1945) providing stability and experience
Substantial asset base of $151.8 million and 17,200+ members indicating financial health and stability
Comprehensive digital banking suite including mobile apps for iOS/Android, online banking, bill pay, mobile payments, and remote deposit capture
Full-service mortgage lending including fixed-rate and adjustable-rate options across multiple terms
Member support services including financial counseling, financial education workshops, and tax preparation assistance
Competitive auto lending for new and used vehicles with stated competitive rates
Active community engagement through Q&A forum and financial wellness content
cons: Severely limited branch network with only 1 branch and 3 ATMs, restricting in-person access for non-local members
Mixed member reviews with one reviewer reporting money mishandling and rude staff, indicating potential service quality inconsistencies
No geographic flexibility—state charter limited to Indiana with no apparent shared branching or surcharge-free ATM networks mentioned
Small employee base (72 staff) relative to 17,200 members may impact service response times
Lobby currently closed according to website, creating access barriers for members needing in-person services
best_for: Richmond and Connersville, Indiana residents seeking local community credit union banking with personalized service
Indiana members looking for comprehensive lending products including mortgages, auto loans, and business loans in a traditional credit union structure
Savers prioritizing member-owned institutions and financial education over convenience of large branch networks
Small business owners in Indiana needing credit union-based business lending and personal banking integration
diagnosis: Natco Credit Union is best for Indiana residents—particularly in the Richmond and Connersville areas—who prioritize local, member-owned banking and can access the single branch location. The main caveat is the extremely limited physical footprint (1 branch, 3 ATMs) and mixed member reviews regarding service quality, which prospective members should carefully evaluate before joining. Digital banking tools partially offset location constraints but cannot replace in-person service accessibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
